headline: Emergency Psychiatry Beds at Only 2.1 Per 100,000 Population
context: Acute psychiatric bed shortage means patients in crisis are often held in general wards or turned away entirely.

cards.big: Malaysia has only 2.1 psychiatric beds per 100,000 versus WHO minimum of 5 per 100,000
cards.sub: Psychiatric emergencies are the fastest-growing category of hospital crisis presentations

cards.big: 45% of psychiatric emergency patients wait more than 12 hours for bed placement
cards.sub: Extended emergency department stays worsen patient condition and delay treatment

cards.big: Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta built in 1911 still houses 25% of all psychiatric beds nationally
cards.sub: Colonial-era institutional model has not been replaced by modern community alternatives

cards.big: Italy closed all psychiatric hospitals in 1978 replacing them with community mental health centers
cards.sub: Deinstitutionalization with adequate community investment produced better patient outcomes

cards.big: A mental health system with roots in a 1911 asylum needs more than renovation—it needs reinvention
Should Malaysia build crisis stabilization units in every state to replace asylum-model psychiatry
